preamble: The shift from the voiced labiodental fricative [v] to the bilabial nasal [m] is a form of nasalization and substitution grounded in their shared labial place of articulation, commonly observed in specific language families and child language acquisition.

examples.note: Part of the broader labial sound shifts and correspondences between fricatives, stops, and nasals in Nakh dialects.
Occasional nasal substitution in early phonological development where labial sounds are generalized.

sources: Phonological studies on Nakh language correspondences.
